'''Ben Westwood''' (born 25 July 1981) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer, who played as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} in the [[Super League]]. He played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level, and also represented [[England Knights]] and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]].

He started his professional career with the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]] before moving to the [[Warrington Wolves]] in 2002. He made over 400 appearances during his 18 years at the club, and was part of Warrington's [[2009 Challenge Cup|2009]], [[2010 Challenge Cup|2010]] and [[2012 Challenge Cup]] winning teams.

==Background==
Westwood was born in [[Normanton, West Yorkshire|Normanton]], [[West Yorkshire, England]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
He began his career at local team [[Normanton (rugby league)|Normanton Knights]], before being scouted for Wakefield Trinity Wildcats.

===Club career===
====Wakefield Trinity Wildcats====
Westwood spent three seasons with Wakefield Trinity Wildcats between 1999 and 2002.

====Warrington Wolves====
In June 2002, Westwood was transferred to [[Warrington Wolves]] for a fee of £60,000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Westwood signed until end of 2004 |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5267468.westwood-signed-until-end-of-2004/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=10 September 2023 |date=7 June 2002}}</ref>

A talismanic player for Warrington who has been top tackler at the club for the past two seasons and top tackler out of marker for the past three, Westwood was Warrington's Players' Player of the Year and Fans' Player of the Year in 2007. A conversion from the centers to the pack at the start of 2005 transformed his career and pushed him to international honors.

He was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] for 2008's [[Super League XIII]].<ref name="2008 engage Super League Dream Team">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|title=2008 engage Super League Dream Team|publisher=Super League|date=15 September 2008|access-date=26 September 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080918211731/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=11755|archive-date=18 September 2008|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

Westwood played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref>

He also enjoyed a good year in 2011 despite injuries, when the Wire topped the league table.

He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

He played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>

He played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]],<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref> and played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

In July 2019, Westwood announced he would be retiring at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Ben Westwood: Warrington Wolves forward to retire at end of 2019 season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49178974 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=10 September 2023 |date=31 July 2019}}</ref>

===Representative career===
[[File:Ben Westwood 2008.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Westwood in action for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]]]]
He was a substitute for Yorkshire against Lancashire in the second game of the expanded County of Origin series in 2002.<ref name="A timeline of Ben Westwood's highlights in a Warrington Wolves shirt">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/15479334.Westwood_joins_Wire_s_top_10_appearance_makers|title=A timeline of Ben Westwood's highlights in a Warrington Wolves shirt|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=17 August 2017|access-date=25 November 2017}}</ref>
 
He won a call up to the Great Britain standby squad for the 2006 Tri-Nations.

In September 2008 he was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="Myler gets England call">{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=29 September 2008|access-date=3 October 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=7 October 2008|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> and in October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England]] squad.<ref name="Purdham earns World Cup call-up">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|title=Purdham earns World Cup call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20081009081658/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|archive-date= 9 October 2008 |url-status= live}}</ref>

By 2011, Westwood has established himself within the England squad and appeared in all of England's [[2011 Four Nations]] matches. His performances in the tournament were noted as characteristics of a hard-working and aggressive back-rower who thrives on intensity.

He was named in the England squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].
